etymology.description: semantic 牛(牛) (niú, "cow") + phonetic 勿(勿) (wù) -> thing; object
etymology.interpretation: The character 物(物) (wù, "thing; object") combines 牛(牛) (niú, "cow") as the semantic component, signifying tangible entities or physical objects, with 勿(勿) (wù) as the phonetic component to provide the sound. The usage of 牛(牛), representing cattle, suggests that the meanings originally included tangible, concrete items before broadening to general objects.

examples.chinese: 这个物品很贵重(這個物品很貴重)。
我在寻找一个特别的东西(我在尋找一個特別的東西)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài xúnzhǎo yī gè tèbié de dōngxi.
examples.english: I am looking for a special thing.

examples.chinese: 每样物品都有其用途(每樣物品都有其用途)。
examples.pinyin: Měi yàng wùpǐn dōu yǒu qí yòngtú.
